name: claude-hooks-ups-pipeline
tags: pipeline, graph, UPS, UserPromptSubmit, langgraph, nodes, fan-out, parallel, session graph, topology, active task, task context, task context creation, context injection, tier1, tier2, how task context works
body:
When a task is active, UPS builds task context by running 4 nodes in parallel (tier 1): load_task_history, load_task_code, load_related_tasks, load_related_commits. (load_active_review was removed June 2026 when the review system was removed.)

Two parallel fan-out tiers:
Tier 1 (task context nodes): load_active_task → [load_task_history ∥ load_task_code ∥ load_related_tasks ∥ load_related_commits]
Tier 2 (context nodes): fan-in → [cwd_domain_detect ∥ load_memories ∥ score_tools] → set_prompt_id → log_task_events → END
Without an active task, all tier-1 nodes return empty dicts and tier-2 runs context-only.
